task_completion.description: Identify spam in the inbox and move it to the Junk folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the email client's folder pane or message list. The Inbox should appear free of obvious spam, and/or the Junk folder should show an increased message count (around 17-20 total, as 10 were pre-existing and 10 were planted in the inbox).

task_completion.description: Create a new IMAP folder named 'Spam-Incidents'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the left sidebar or folder tree in the email client. A folder exactly named 'Spam-Incidents' must be visible.

task_completion.description: Move at least 3 egregious spam examples into the 'Spam-Incidents' folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Spam-Incidents' folder being selected or having a message count indicator showing at least 3 emails inside it.

task_completion.description: Draft an email to the security team with the correct recipient and subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open email composition window. The 'To' field must be exactly 'security-response@company.com' and the 'Subject' field must be exactly 'Spam Filter Bypass Incident Report'.

task_completion.description: Include the required details in the email body: subject lines, total count, and spam description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the text area of the drafted email. It must contain a list of spam subject lines, a stated total count of spam found (which should be 10), and a description of the types of spam present.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical email client to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a standard GUI email client (like Thunderbird, Evolution, or a webmail interface) being used to move emails and draft the report, rather than terminal commands or a plain text editor.

integrity.description: The reported spam subjects match actual emails from the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The subject lines typed into the incident report should look like real spam subjects (from the Apache SpamAssassin corpus) and correspond to emails seen in the Inbox/Junk folders, not generic hallucinated text.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the email application rather than directly modifying mail files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of clicking, dragging, or using the email client's menus to create folders and move emails, rather than terminal windows showing 'mv' or 'mkdir' commands in the maildir.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses real email data from the Apache SpamAssassin Public Corpus. Exactly 10 spam emails were planted in the Inbox (mixed with 40 legitimate ham emails). The initial Junk folder already contained 10 pre-existing spam emails. The agent must create a folder named 'Spam-Incidents' and move at least 3 spam emails there. The incident report email must be addressed to 'security-response@company.com' with the subject 'Spam Filter Bypass Incident Report'. The total count of identified spam reported in the email body should be exactly 10.
